findings.message: No version floor specified for `requests`. Every other dependency in this file carries an explicit `>=X.Y` constraint; `requests` is pinned to no minimum, meaning any version — including pre-2.32.0 releases that contain CVE-2024-35195 (credential-leaking redirect) — is acceptable to the resolver.
findings.suggestion: Add a version floor: `"requests>=2.32"` to stay above the CVE-2024-35195 fix and be consistent with the rest of the manifest.

findings.message: `requests` is a synchronous, blocking HTTP client. The project runs on an async-first stack (FastAPI + uvicorn). Calling `requests` directly from an async route handler will block the event loop and degrade server throughput under any concurrency.
findings.suggestion: If the call site is inside an `async def` handler, either use `httpx.AsyncClient` (async-native) or wrap the call in `asyncio.to_thread(...)` to avoid blocking the event loop. If the call site is in a sync worker this is fine, but the use case should be documented.

findings.message: `httpx` is already present in the `[dependency-groups] dev` group and covers the same HTTP-client use case with both sync and async APIs. Adding `requests` as a production dependency when an equivalent library already exists in the project increases the dependency surface unnecessarily.
findings.suggestion: Consider promoting `httpx>=0.28` from the dev group to the main `dependencies` list instead of adding `requests`. This removes the duplicate, keeps the async-compatible client on the production path, and avoids the event-loop-blocking concern above.
